rulings.Case Title: JOHN COYLE VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (B/P)AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Thursday, September 16, 2010 in Department 220 at 9:30 a.m., Line 3. Defendant Goodrich Corporation¿s motion for summary judgment is denied. rulings.Case Title: LEROY CABRAL VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (BP) AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Thursday, September 16, 2010 in Department 220 at 9:30 a.m., Line 5. Defendant Pacific Mechanical Corp.¿s motion for summary judgment is denied and its alternative motion for summary adjudication is denied as to plaintiff¿s first (negligence), second (strict liability) and fifth (contractor liability) causes of action and granted as to plaintiff¿s third cause of action (false representation). By failing to present plaintiff¿s responses to contention discovery with its moving papers, defendant did not satisfy its burden of production that plaintiff does not possess and cannot reasonably obtain evidence that plaintiff was exposed to asbestos-containing materials attributable to defendant. Because plaintiff did not have the opportunity to respond to the evidentiary materials submitted with the reply papers, the court declines to consider those materials. Plaintiff concedes that his claim for false representation should be dismissed. =(220/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: JOHN BURNS VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (B/P)AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Thursday, September 16, 2010 in Department 220 at 9:30 a.m., Line 6. Defendant Collins Electrical Company, Inc.¿s motion for summary judgment is granted. Defendant sustained its burden of production and plaintiff failed to present evidence creating a triable issue whether he was exposed to asbestos-containing fireproofing attributable to defendant. Plaintiff¿s notice objection is waived as plaintiff opposed on the merits and did not request a continuance or contend he was prejudiced. (See Carlton v. Quint (2000) 77 Cal.App.4th 690, 697.) Defendant¿s objections to Mr. Ay¿s declaration are sustained. =(220/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: JOHN BURNS VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (B/P)AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Thursday, September 16, 2010 in Department 220 at 9:30 a.m., Line 6. Defendant Crane Co.¿s motion for summary judgment is granted. Defendant sustained its burden of production and plaintiff failed to present evidence creating a triable issue whether any exposure he had to asbestos-containing valves attributable to defendant was a substantial factor in causing or contributing to plaintiff¿s asbestos-related injuries. =(220/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: MICHAEL STARR VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (B*P) AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Thursday, September 16, 2010 in Department 220 at 9:30 a.m., Line 12. rulings.Case Title: DENNIS HENRY SCHECHINGER VS. AMERICAN STANDARD, INC. et al
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Thursday, September 16, 2010 in Department 220 at 9:30 a.m., Line 17. Defendant Vornado Realty Trust¿s motion for summary judgment is continued to October 1, 2010 at 9:30 a.m. in Department 220 to allow plaintiff to file supplemental opposition papers based on the depositions of defendant¿s witnesses. Those depositions must occur no later than September 21 and can take place by telephone or videoconferencing. The court will treat plaintiff¿s opposition as a request to amend his complaint to allege that defendant is the successor of and liable for the conduct of the entity that sold asbestos-containing products to plaintiff. and will grant that request and thus defendant¿s motion for summary judgment will turn on whether plaintiff is able to present admissible evidence creating a triable issue on that allegation. Supplemental opposition papers must be filed no later than September 24. Supplemental reply papers must be filed no later than noon on September 29. =(220/HEK)

Supplemental reply papers must be filed no later than noon on September 29. =(220/HEK)
